text: What is a LUT (in plain English)

Because the format is so simple, every serious color tool reads it: Lightroom Cl
Why extract a LUT from a photo
Three scenarios where it's the right move.
First, you shot a series under changing light. A wedding starts in soft afternoo
Second, you love how someone else's photo looks. You see a frame on Instagram or
The quick method (30 seconds)
If you just want the answer:
One. Go to sammapix.com/tools/color-match.
Two. Drop your reference photo in the left panel using From photo mode.
That's it. Everything runs in your browser. The reference never leaves your devi
Step one: pick a strong reference.
The reference photo is the single most important variable. The algorithm samples
Good references: a single dominant lighting condition like golden hour, blue hou
Bad references: photos with multiple competing light sources, blown highlights t
Step two: drop into the generator.
You'll see a progress bar from 5 percent to 100 percent over two to three second
Step three: download the cube.
Step four, optional: apply to a batch in the browser.
If you don't need the cube file itself and just want to apply the look to other
How to use the cube in Lightroom
Lightroom Classic and Camera Raw both support 3D LUTs through their Profile syst
One. Open Lightroom Classic and switch to the Develop module.
Four. In the Profile Browser, click the plus icon in the top-right corner and ch
Five. Select your cube file. Lightroom converts it to an xmp profile internally
To apply the look to a batch: develop one photo with the new profile, right-clic
Premiere Pro: drop a clip on the timeline, open the Lumetri Color panel, expand
Common mistakes to avoid
Mistake one: extracting from a heavily edited reference. If the reference alread
Mistake three: skipping the intensity slider. Most extracted LUTs benefit from b
Mistake four: ignoring contrast and exposure. A LUT remaps colors, not luminance
When LUT extraction is the wrong tool
When you need a signature commercial look. Mastin Labs, VSCO and RNI sell LUT pa
When the photo is heavily damaged. Sampling colors from a scratched, faded or gr
SammaPix LUT Generator is free, extracts from photo, exports cube file.
Lightroom Classic costs ten to twenty dollars per month, does not extract from p
3D LUT Creator from Russia costs 99 dollars, has partial extraction capabilities
IWLTBAP offers a free LUT pack, does not extract, ships pre-made cube files.

The honest take: SammaPix is the fastest free path from "I like this photo's loo
Can you really extract a LUT from just one photo?
Yes. The algorithm samples thousands of colors from the reference, builds a 17 b
What is a cube file exactly?
A plain text 3D color lookup table defined by Adobe's spec. It contains a TITLE
Does the extracted LUT match the original colorist's intent?
Not perfectly. You get the "feel" of the reference's palette, not the exact curv
Yes. Develop module, Profile Browser, plus icon, Import Profiles, select the cub

Are there file size limits or upload fees?
Everything runs in your browser. Nothing leaves your device. No per-photo fees,
